paragraphs.body: We live in a world where women are asked to choose between a bear and a man to be alone in a forest, and the overbearing majority choose the bear. Violence against women has surged throughout history, and there doesn’t seem to be an end to it any time soon. It has come to the point where a situation with a zoo animal would be more reasonable than the horror of a man’s desires.

annotations.text: We live in a world where women are asked to choose between a bear and a man to be alone in a forest, and the overbearing majority choose the bear.
annotations.comment: I love that you're referencing this meme, but a lot of people won't know it or  understand it. Perhaps you could create a longer narrative about this event so that everyone will know exactly what you're talking about?

paragraphs.body: The English language has evolved in many ways. Many words can have multiple meanings, and according to the word used, the thoughts and feelings of the audience morph accordingly. During my English Composition class in my freshman year of university, I learned that I could use my words to illuminate the topics and ideas I wanted to portray in my essay. In this essay, I will guide you through the eyes of women.

annotations.text: The English language has evolved in many ways. Many words can have multiple meanings, and according to the word used, the thoughts and feelings of the audience morph accordingly. During my English Composition class in my freshman year of university, I learned that I could use my words to illuminate the topics and ideas I wanted to portray in my essay. In this essay, I will guide you through the eyes of women.
annotations.comment: I think this section was necessary for your thinking process but unnecessary for the essay, especially because the next paragraph is such a surprising and amazing transition!

paragraphs.body: My role as a woman is to bear. Bear, a verb in the Old English beran, means “to carry, bring; bring forth, give birth to, produce; to endure, to wear.” According to etymonline.com, My role as a woman Is to carry my husband’s child, produce an heir, and bring forth the next generation. My role as a woman is to endure the comments made by men as I walk by and to wear clothing that does not seduce men but not too modestly that I am considered boring. As a woman, my role is to bear.

annotations.text: My role as a woman is to bear. Bear, a verb in the Old English beran, means “to carry, bring; bring forth, give birth to, produce; to endure, to wear.”
annotations.comment: I love love love the way you're using another version of "bear" in this essay. What a fab transition.
This is a great beginning of a definition paragraph. What if you extended it? Got even more concrete and specific? Added layer upon layer from your own life and experience?

paragraphs.body: Bear, as a noun in the Old English bera is a “large carnivorous or omnivorous mammal of the family Ursidae.” According to etymonline.com. a bear the embodiment of the wild, operates primarily on instinct. It does not play games or engage in manipulative behaviors. It acts upon its need for survival. A bear’s actions are straightforward and transparent. It may be dangerous if threatened, but it will not deceive, manipulate, or feed on the emotional vulnerabilities of others. It will leave you alone or respond based on its instincts, which are predictable and rooted in survival.

In contrast, a man, shaped by societal norms and conditioning, creates a web of behaviors. While some genuine men seek to protect or care for women, how they express this can sometimes be clouded. These complexities make a woman feel uncertain about their emotional and physical safety.

annotations.text: In contrast, a man, shaped by societal norms and conditioning, creates a web of behaviors. While some genuine men seek to protect or care for women, how they express this can sometimes be clouded. These complexities make a woman feel uncertain about their emotional and physical safety.
annotations.comment: The voice of the essay shifts, here. Why?

paragraphs.body: After the most recent presidential election, I have come to quarrel with boys who felt the need to argue with my views. They went to the extent of calling one of the candidates an Antichrist and using the restriction of the bodily autonomy of women as their argument to prove their point. As a response to his blasphemy, I had to remind these boys of their privilege. I told him that a man who has no idea what It feels like to be a woman has no right to talk about what women get to do with their bodies. As a woman, I’ve grown tired of being expected to bear these conversations, to carry the emotional weight of educating those who cannot or will not listen. We are not a policy that can be voted upon nor government property.

annotations.text: As a woman, I’ve grown tired of being expected to bear these conversations, to carry the emotional weight of educating those who cannot or will not listen. We are not a policy that can be voted upon nor government property.
annotations.comment: Wow. So cool. Another use of the word "bear."

paragraphs.body: I have observed my mother and picked up her habit of collecting all the receipts she has received. I asked her why she collected a useless piece of paper, but she told me it was the most important piece of information that you could save. A receipt is a piece of paper that is very thin and easy to crinkle. The paper. Faintly rough to the touch, it smells faintly of ink and a smidge of stale air. The words on the receipt always look faded, as there always seems to be a lack of ink. The print is faded in places, and the numbers and words are etched unevenly. You can hear the soft rustling as you search the bottom of your bag. The crackling sound of the paper resisting to rip into two. Usually, receipts come with the stomach-dropping feeling of reflecting on the amount of money that was spent. A record of all your purchases. Receipts come in long, small, and many different sizes and fonts. They bear the needs and wants of everyone.

annotations.comment: I see your attempts to synthesize your word "bear" and your object, "the receipt." Let's talk about this! I wonder if the object is exactly the right one, now, as your ideas have shifted and grown throughout the writing process. If so, how can the essay introduce the idea (and metaphor) of the receipt earlier in the essay?
annotations.context_left: and easy to crinkle. The paper. Faintly rough to the touch, it smells faintly of ink and a smidge of

paragraphs.body: A receipt, proof of something given, something exchanged, something that has been taken. It holds both the value and emptiness of what has been spent, much like the emotional labor women perform every day. A receipt may seem small and insignificant, but it carries with it the documentation of what is owed, even if that debt is never fully paid. Women often bear the weight of the world in silence, their worth unacknowledged but their burden unrelenting, much like that receipt that is crumpled at the bottom of your shopping bags waiting to be recognized.

Henrietta Lacks is a receipt for her eternal legacy. She was diagnosed with cervical cancer, and without her consent, researchers stole samples of her cancer cells. Her cells kept reproducing even after her death, ultimately making them immortal. Her cells have made breaking new scientific discoveries. Yet her story has brought the question of ethics to patient privacy only recently, exposing the mistreatment of people of color in healthcare.

Sarah Hyland is a receipt for the ability to survive domestic violence and come out the other end as more than she was before, finding the ability to love again. She spoke up about what courage means to her and reflected that “it takes courage to wake up every morning and face the world knowing that people may have no idea what you are going through.” Her story encourages domestic abuse survivors to keep their heads high and never underestimate the power of knowing that they are not alone.

Choosing a bear rather than a man in a forest serves as a pitiful metaphor, serving as a reflection of the complexities and emotional burdens women carry daily. A bear is a form of clarity and predictability, which is often absent in human interactions, especially in relationships shaped by expectations put on by society. The choice to feel safer with a bear than with a man is not about the literal danger posed by one or the other. It’s the safety women seek in a world that often treats them as objects to be controlled or ignored. Women throughout history have been conditioned to bear the weight of expectations, the emotional triumphs of relationships, or the physical and psychological toll of gendered violence. This bearing, while a fundamental part of the experience of a woman, often goes unnoticed, unacknowledged, and unvalued. Using the power of languages, we explored the deeply ingrained roles women are expected to fulfill.

annotations.text: Women throughout history have been conditioned to bear the weight of expectations, the emotional triumphs of relationships, or the physical and psychological toll of gendered violence.

paragraphs.body: The receipt, though small and forgetful, also represents an enduring record of exchange. Its ink faded, the paper crinkled, yet the memory of the transaction remains. A mirror of the experience of women who, though often unseen and unheard, leave lasting impressions on the world through their sacrifices and resilience. The receipt may seem like a temporary record, but it is a reminder of the ongoing exchange that women make with the world. More giving than receiving. A receipt is a story, an experience, a possibility of the constant balancing of roles, the juggling of family and career, and the work that sustains households and relationships. annotations.comment: The voice shifts again, here. Whether you used A.I. or not, it sounds like an A.I. voice, and--therefore--is less authentic, compelling, and effective as your own. Let's talk.

This essay has some fabulous bones! Wow. The way you use "bear" as an organizing structural motif and the ways you "essay" this word from so many different perspectives is amazing. I also see you working to synthesize the word with the object, though I think there's still some more to explore in order to make that relationship cohesive. I'm also wondering about the voice of this essay. There are several parts of the essay that don't read as authentically your own. I'm not sure if you're using A.I. to create this essay, if you ran out of time, or if you simply don't trust your own voice and writing enough to use it on the page. We'll have to talk. The irony is that your perspectives, your ideas. and your essay as a whole, is much more powerful when you write it in your own voice. Your voice is so compelling! Your ideas are so meaningful! I look forward to talking with you :)
